Twitter needs leadership that doesn't think someone else can run the company better than they can. https://www.cnbc.com/...

Twitter users are up +16% yet revenue is down -1% year over year. That is shocking. This is worse than Snapchat's earnings where the combination of the economy and Apple ATT hurt worse than expected. I'd have expected some revenue growth given that much user growth. This is bad.

Twitter Reports Surprising Drop in Revenue Amid Elon Musk Fight Social-media company blames dip on advertising weakness and uncertainty related to its pending $44 billion takeover deal https://www.wsj.com/...

Twitter cited “advertising industry headwinds associated with the macroenvironment as well as uncertainty related to the pending acquisition of Twitter.” Won't host an earnings conference call because of the pending transaction. https://www.wsj.com/...

Twitter earnings are out. They show the company's user base is growing, but revenues aren't. Twitter apportions some of the blame to “uncertainty” surrounding the Musk deal. $TWTR down 2% in premarket. https://www.cnbc.com/...

Twitter earnings are out and they missed on every number • Revenue: $1.18B vs $1.32B expected • mDAU: 237.8M vs 238.08M expected • lost -$344M vs +$30M profit last year Twitter blamed the economy and uncertainty created by Elon Musk on missed numbers. https://www.cnbc.com/...

Snap grew DAU by +18% year over year and revenue by +13%. Twitter grew mDAU by +16% year over year but revenue is down -1%. This doesn't help the narrative that something is wonky with Twitter's mDAU numbers. Best case scenario is their ads business sucks worse than believed.

Facebook and Instagram are becoming entertainment platforms. This Musk thing will kill Twitter one way or another. Is 2022 going to be the end of “social networking” as a thing? Except LinkedIn, of course. LinkedIn will somehow outlive us all https://twitter.com/...

Seeing the bad earnings numbers from $SNAP and $TWTR due in part to Apple's ATT changes is the tip of the iceberg of the economic damage. I still think if you wanted to invest (not financial advice) in doing cool shit, I'd be on Snap where there are ways to transform what you do on social into art, fashion and surreal moments. There is not that much fun out there except maybe Jortcore. https://adage.com/...

Meta revenue growth in September is expected to be 4%. $META likely won't be that bad, but prepare that there is risk.
Waiting for the call. Good news is DAUs were up 18% (347 million) in Q2 2022, an increase by +18% y/y (or +54 million).

FCC chair asks carriers to explain how they use geolocation data, as Congress debates a law that may preempt the FCC from regulating carriers' privacy practices
Carrier location data usage again under investigation, after promises broken
FCC probes 15 leading mobile carriers about location data in wake of abortion ruling
FCC chair presses top mobile carriers on data privacy practices

Our phones know our location at any given moment. This geolocation data is especially sensitive. It's a record of where we've been and who we are. It's why the FCC took action today to probe the data #privacy practices of mobile carriers. https://www.fcc.gov/...

The FCC ordered mobile firms to disclose how they're using location data. Meanwhile a >$200M fine by FCC in 2020 vs all major carriers for selling location data still pending. Now a new bill would preempt the FCC from regulating carrier privacy practices https://arstechnica.com/...

It's my duty as @FCC Chairwoman to protect consumers. Mobile internet service providers are uniquely situated to capture data about their own subscribers, including their identity, geolocation data, app usage, and web browsing data and habits. https://www.fcc.gov/...

you can see some telecom experts issuing early warnings about this, but they risk being ignored by a “big tech” obsessed press and policy apparatus that stopped caring about telecom policy several years ago https://twitter.com/...

We're delighted to announce that Meta has committed ongoing financial support for the Board, including a new $150 million contribution to the Oversight Board Trust to support our operations. 🧵

“Today the Oversight Board Trust announced that Meta has made a commitment that provides for ongoing financial support for the Oversight Board. As part of that commitment, the company will make a $150 million contribution to the Trust.” https://oversightboard.com/...
Stephen Neal, Chairperson of the Trust, says this is “a vote of confidence in the work of the Board.” Read more here: https://oversightboard.com/...

I knocked Coinbase for its timing in releasing this today for PR and still feel that way. That said, the blog post makes some valid points about how the SEC continues to mishandle the space https://blog.coinbase.com/...

Today the DOJ and SEC brought actions against a former Coinbase employee, his brother, and his friend for allegedly trading ahead of listing announcements. The SEC's action here is particularly relevant for securities analysis. I will walk through the details here. 🧵
@propelforward But the SEC does: it asserts that several tokens in which these guys allegedly traded are “crypto asset securities.” https://www.sec.gov/...
The question, however: https://www.sec.gov/... Since the SEC calls these securities, and they were clearly unregistered, are they going to go after all the clowns behind them? https://twitter.com/...

The SEC has been really busy lately The SEC has arrested and charged a former Coinbase employee with wire fraud. Now In the SEC complaint, it mentions nine tokens and says they are all securities. AMP, RLY, DDX, XYO, RGT, LCX, POWR, DFX, KROM https://www.theblock.co/... https://twitter.com/...

We are tantalizingly close to a bunch of VC firms being charged with securities fraud for their insider trading of tokens during the bull market. It's incredible that this is even they did things to make this possible let alone being probable at this point.
here's the complaint pdf. Howey analysis starts on page 23 of the doc https://www.sec.gov/...

Instagram-based thrift stores in India, Bangladesh, Pakistan, and Nepal are making used clothes popular, even desirable, among young shoppers

Years ago, running a successful thrift store would have been unthinkable in most of South Asia, where buying used clothes was frowned upon, but Instagram has flipped this belief on its head. @helenliwrites & @kisha_ravi tell the story for @restofworld 👚👖https://restofworld.o rg/ ...

Across South Asia, the changing attitudes of Gen Z and millennials have propelled a boom in the secondhand clothing market. Used clothes, once looked down upon, are flying off Instagram's digital shelves. @helenliwrites & @kisha_ravi for @restofworld https://restofworld.org/...

Thrifting wasn't cool in South Asia because no one wanted “used clothes.” But now, Instagram's aesthetically designed grids have made it desirable, and these stores are frequently running out of stock - @helenliwrites and @kisha_ravi for @restofworld https://restofworld.org/...

“Why is @Amazon acquiring @OneMedical bad news?” Let's explore. 1. Horizontal integration at Amazon scale of a company that now has the right to access my medical records under HIPAA opens up a huge problem for abuse.
I do not see any strategic logic to Amazon getting into healthcare clinics.
NEWS: @SenAmyKlobuchar is calling on the FTC to “thoroughly investigate” Amazon's $3.9 billion deal to buy health care provider One Medical https://twitter.com/...
4. Related to pressure, if you openly criticize Amazon are you sure that some upset exec won't go browsing through your medical file? I mean *REALLY* sure? I'm not.

One Medical in is about 18 cities — mostly large metros. They run about 125 locations focused on primary care. (They pitch to employers and say they have 8000+ clients) This means Amazon will have: -doc/clinic network -big mail order pharmacy -distribution framework
Amazon just bought my doctor's office. Totally normal and not at all a dystopian nightmare. https://twitter.com/...
I am less than pleased to learn that Amazon is acquiring my doctor. Time to figure out how to delete my medical records. https://twitter.com/...
The dystopia vibes refuse to take a break https://twitter.com/...

Just fyi that Amazon now has: - telemedicine / brick & mortar primary care - a diagnostics effort - pharmacy - grocery - stealth bio R&D - health tracking wearables What's left? A hospital? https://techcrunch.com/...
I wonder much will a Prime membership cost in 2030 and what will all the new features be? Access to healthcare part of Prime?

New unpopular opinion: would *love* for Amazon to be my doctor. It can't possibly be worse than the mess of engaging with carriers/providers today. Sneezing in the direction of a hospital financially ruins people, often unknowingly.

.@Amazon is going to expand their mass, personal data collection scheme with the purchase of @onemedical. This is bad for competition in health care, and opens a terrifying new frontier in surveillance of Americans by private corporations. https://www.wsj.com/...

1. Reinforces that Amazon, Apple & Google want a slice of healthcare TAM. 2. M&A is back on after the tech wreck. I expect more to follow. 3.Take out valuations are pointing back to pre-pandemic levels. Amazon's paying 18% below where shares in Jan of 2020 at the IPO.

The function of a rational health care system is to provide quality care to all in a cost-effective way, not make billionaires like Jeff Bezos even richer. At a time of growing concentration of ownership, the Justice Department must deny Amazon's acquisition of One Medical. https://twitter.com/...

As far as I'm concerned, Amazon is the only Big Tech player with real and ambitions designs in the health tech space plus top down support. Acquisitions don't happen often and require many layers of approvals.

US consumer banking is also garbage by developed world standards (WFT is a ‘voided check’?) but I can see Apple getting into that (of course, define 'bank'). But healthcare seems an order of magnitude further from any tech company corporate strengths.

Antitrust enforcers should block this deal. For one, it would give Amazon access to a cache of intimate data that would entrench its dominance. Second, it would allow Amazon to gain structural power in yet another major sector. This is why we have anti-monopoly laws.1/ https://twitter.com/...
